text: The AEB pedestrian system shall detect partially occluded pedestrians at night within 40 m for ego vehicle speeds up to 50 km/h and verify the result by scenario test evidence.
The AEB validation campaign shall include at least 30 night-time pedestrian crossing scenarios with low-beam headlights, wet road surface, and measured illumination below 10 lux.
The AEB system shall react quickly when a pedestrian is detected in front of the vehicle.
expected_quality_issues: too vague; missing verification method; missing measurable threshold; missing ODD condition

text: The lane keeping assist system shall warn the driver within 500 ms when unintended lane departure risk is detected at speeds between 60 km/h and 130 km/h on clearly marked roads.
The system shall monitor lane marking confidence at runtime and record a diagnostic event when confidence remains below 70% for more than 2 seconds.
The perception dataset shall include at least 5,000 labeled vulnerable road user samples across night, rain, glare, and partial occlusion conditions.
The perception software shall publish object detection confidence, object class, bounding box, timestamp, and sensor source for every tracked vulnerable road user at a minimum rate of 10 Hz.

The minimal risk maneuver controller shall transition the vehicle to a safe stop within 8 seconds when the perception subsystem reports unavailable object detection for more than 1 second in the active ODD.
Each safety requirement shall be linked to one hazard, one safety goal, one verification method, and at least one objective evidence artifact before release approval.
expected_quality_issues: missing measurable threshold; missing ODD condition; missing linked hazard; missing linked safety goal

text: The deployed system shall store safety monitor events with timestamp, vehicle speed, ODD state, triggering condition, and software version for 100% of safety-relevant interventions.
